title: Changes in the market for loanable funds
description: Practice what you have learned about how changes in the supply and demand for loanable funds can impact interest rates,
topic_path: Changes in the market for loanable funds

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the result of a government moving from a budget deficit to a budget surplus?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real interest rates decrease
Bond prices decrease
Aggregate demand increases
The money supply increases
Government borrowing increases

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph below shows the effect of a change in the market for loanable funds.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best describes an event that could cause the change shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots three labeled lines and two points on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled real interest rate, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript L F. One upward-sloping line from the bottom left to the top right is labeled S subscript L F subscript 1. Two downward-sloping lines from top left to bottom right are labeled D subscript L F subscript 1 and D subscript L F subscript 2, with D subscript L F subscript 2 above D subscript L F subscript 1. Two points mark the intersections of S subscript L F subscript 1 with D subscript L F subscript 1 and D subscript L F subscript 2. A horizontal dashed line extends from r subscript 1 on the vertical axis to the lower intersection. Another horizontal dashed line extends from r subscript 2 to the upper intersection. Vertical dashed lines drop from each intersection to the horizontal axis.

choices.content: A government runs a budget deficit
Foreign governments buy more of this country's bonds.
People save more.
People save less.
Firms want to invest less in plant and equipment.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would be a result of a government moving from a balanced budget to a budget deficit?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Interest rates increase
Bond prices increase
Aggregate demand decreases
The money supply decreases
Government borrowing decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Burginville had a balanced budget, but a recession decreased tax revenues.

**Which of the following should be used to show the effect of the decrease in tax revenues on interest rates in Burginville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The market for loanable funds
The money market
The aggregate demand–aggregate supply, or AD-AS, model
The production possibilities curve
The circular flow diagram

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Noobistan has introduced a new pension program that guarantees every retired citizen a minimum income. As a result, citizens are saving less for retirement.

**Which of the following best describes the impact of this statement on the market for loanable funds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Less savings means that the supply of loanable funds decreases, increasing the interest rate.
Less savings means that the supply of loanable funds increases, decreasing the interest rate.
Less savings means that the demand for loanable funds decreases, decreasing the interest rate.
Less savings means that the demand for loanable funds increases, increasing the interest rate.
Less savings means that the supply of loanable funds increases and the demand for loanable funds increases, which has an indeterminate effect on interest rates.

assessment_items.question_content:
Citizens in Fredland are saving more.

**Which of the following best describes the impact of this statement on the market for loanable funds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of loanable funds increases and the real interest rate decreases.
The supply of loanable funds decreases and the real interest rate increases.
The demand for loanable funds increases and the real interest rate increases.
The demand for loanable funds decreases and the real interest rate decreases.
The demand for loanable funds decreases and the supply of loanable funds decreases and the effect on the interest rate is indeterminate.

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Marthaland had a balanced budget, but an economic boom increased tax revenues.

**Which of the following should be used to show the effect of the increase in tax revenues on interest rates in Marthaland?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The market for loanable funds
The money market
The aggregate demand–aggregate supply, or AD-AS, model
The production possibilities curve
The circular flow diagram

assessment_items.question_content:
Princess Buttercup has granted an investment tax credit in Florin.

**How will the market for loanable funds be impacted and what will the effect be on the interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for loanable funds will increase; the interest rate will increase
The demand for loanable funds will decrease; the interest rate will not change
The supply of loanable funds will increase; the interest rate will decrease
The supply of loanable funds will decrease; the interest rate will not change
Both the supply and demand for loanable funds will increase; the effect on the interest rate is indeterminate

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that Noobistan is a closed economy. As a result of increased spending on smartphone games, national savings in Noobistan has decreased.

**How will the market for loanable funds be impacted,  and what will the effect be on the interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of loanable funds will decrease; the interest rate will increase
The demand for loanable funds will increase; the interest rate will increase
The demand for loanable funds will decrease; the interest rate will not change
The supply of loanable funds will decrease; the interest rate will not change
Both the supply and demand for loanable funds will increase; the effect on the interest rate is indeterminate

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following leads to a higher real interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An investment tax credit
An increase in saving behavior.
Less investment by firms.
More savings from foreign countries.
A decrease in the desire to borrow money.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following leads to a lower real interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The national budget moves from a deficit to a surplus.
People save less.
Investment demand by firms increases.
Foreign savers view the market as risky.
People want to borrow more money.

assessment_items.question_content:
People in Hamsterville are looking forward to a long, fun-filled retirement and are saving more money to make sure they can go on trips around the world when they retire. 

**Which of the following best illustrates the impact of this statement on the market for loanable funds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
People in Hamsterville are looking forward to a long, fun-filled retirement and are saving more money to make sure they can go on trips around the world when they retire. 

**What happens to the interest rate and long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) as a result of what happens to the interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Interest rate decreases; LRAS increases
Interest rate increases; LRAS decreases
Interest rate increases; LRAS increases
Interest rate decreases; LRAS is unaffected
Interest rate doesn't change; LRAS doesn't change

assessment_items.question_content:
In response to a particularly exciting speech by the president of Hamsterville, business optimism is at an all time high, and the demand for capital has increased.

**What will be the impact of this increase in the demand for capital on the market for loanable funds and the stock of capital in Hamsterville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Demand for loanable funds will increase; stock of capital will increase.
Demand for loanable funds will increase; stock of capital will decrease.
Supply of loanable funds will increase; stock of capital will not be affected.
Supply of loanable funds will decrease; stock of capital will increase.
Demand and supply of loanable funds will increase; stock of capital will not be affected.

assessment_items.question_content:
In response to a particularly severe stock market crash in Burginville, business optimism is at an all-time low, and the demand for capital has decreased.

**What will be the impact of a decrease in the demand for capital on the market for loanable funds and the stock of capital in Burginville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Demand for loanable funds will decrease; stock of capital will decrease.
Demand for loanable funds will decrease; stock of capital will increase.
Supply of loanable funds will increase; stock of capital will not be affected.
Supply of loanable funds will decrease; stock of capital will increase.
Demand and supply of loanable funds will decrease; stock of capital will not be affected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the impact of a decrease in the demand for capital on the interest rate and the quantity of loans made?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Interest rate decreases; quantity of loans made decreases.
Interest rate decreases; quantity of loans made is not affected.
Interest rate increases; quantity of loans made decreases.
Interest rate increases; quantity of loans made increases.
Interest rate increases; quantity of loans made decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What relationship is illustrated using the demand for loanable funds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Borrowers respond to a decrease in real interest rates by borrowing more money.
Savers respond to an increase in interest rates by saving more money.
Investors respond to an increase in interest rates by investing more money.
The central bank responds to higher interest rates by supplying more money.
Individuals respond to lower interest rates by increasing the amount of money they hold.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Central Bank of Hamsterville and the government of Hamsterville are trying to control inflation by engaging in contractionary monetary policy and contractionary fiscal policy, respectively. Assume that the budget of Hamsterville was balanced prior to engaging in contractionary fiscal policy.

**What will be the impact of contractionary fiscal policy and contractionary monetary policy on real gross domestic product(GDP) and the interest rate of Hamsterville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Contractionary monetary policy actions, such as selling bonds, decrease the money supply.
Contractionary monetary policy actions increase interest rates. This lowers the investment category of real GDP.
Contractionary fiscal policy lowers GDP because it reduces the government spending category of GDP.
When governments decrease spending, this decreases the demand for loanable funds, lowering the interest rate.
Because contractionary monetary and fiscal policies have opposite effects on interest rates, the effect on interest rates is indeterminate.

choices.content: Real GDP will decrease; impact on interest rates will be indeterminate.
Real GDP will decrease; interest rates will decrease.
Real GDP will decrease; interest rates will increase.
Effect on real GDP will be indeterminate; effect on interest rates will be indeterminate.
Real GDP will increase; interest rates will increase.

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Hamsterville has engaged in expansionary fiscal policy, and the central bank has engaged in expansionary monetary policy. Assume that there is not complete crowding out.

**What will be the effect of these policies on real gross domestic product (GDP) and interest rates in Hamsterville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real GDP will increase; impact on interest rates will be indeterminate.
Real GDP will increase; interest rates will increase.
Real GDP will increase; interest rates will decrease.
Effect on real GDP will be indeterminate; effect on interest rates will be indeterminate.
Real GDP will decrease; interest rates will increase.

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Burginville currently has a balanced budget. Rampant consumer spending in Burginville has led to a decrease in national savings, which has affected real interest rates in Burginville. 

**Which action could the government of Burginville take to offset the effect of a decrease in national savings on real interest rates?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Run a budget surplus.
Run a budget deficit.
Increase the money supply.
Decrease the money supply.
Increase government spending.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below shows the result of a change in the market for loanable funds. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following is the most likely to have caused the shift shown here?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane displays three straight lines and two labeled intersections. The vertical axis is labeled real interest rate, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript L F, both increasing in equal increments. One downward-sloping line is labeled D subscript L F. Two upward-sloping lines are labeled S subscript L F subscript 1 and S subscript L F subscript 2, with S subscript L F subscript 2 above S subscript L F subscript 1. The intersection of D subscript L F with S subscript L F subscript 1 occurs at interest rate r subscript 1 and quantity Q subscript L F subscript 1. The intersection with S subscript L F subscript 2 occurs at r subscript 2 and Q subscript L F subscript 2. Dashed lines extend from each point to both axes.

choices.content: Domestic saving decreases.
The government runs a budget surplus.
Optimism by firms increases investment in physical capital.
Foreign savers view this country as a desirable place to send assets.
Firms invest less in plant and equipment
